sourceMovie.title: A Cure for Wellness
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: A Cure for Wellness (2017) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Gore Verbinski's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Saw
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Cure for Wellness clicked for you, Saw (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: 28 Days Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Cure for Wellness clicked for you, 28 Days Later (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Cure for Wellness?
faq.answer: Try Saw, 28 Days Later, Cube—each shares DNA with A Cure for Wellness while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Cure for Wellness?
faq.answer: A Cure for Wellness (2017) is best described as Horror, Mystery, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Cure for Wellness worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
